message:
fix(transport): make EndpointState atomic and fix update_endpoint_state no-op

EndpointState converted to repr(u8) enum with AtomicU8 storage, enabling
lock-free concurrent state transitions. The state field on Endpoint is now
private, accessed through state()/set_state() methods and a with_state()
builder. All 14 call sites across LoadBalancer, RoutingTable, SymbolRouter,
and SymbolDispatcher updated from direct field access (e.state) to the
accessor (e.state()).

Fixed update_endpoint_state() which was a complete no-op: it checked if the
endpoint existed but never actually applied the new state. Now calls
endpoint.set_state(state) within the read lock (safe because AtomicU8 allows
interior mutability).

Fixed release_connection() which used fetch_sub(1) unconditionally, causing
underflow to u32::MAX when called with zero active connections. Now uses
fetch_update with saturating_sub(1) to clamp at zero.

Added tests:
- test_endpoint_release_connection_saturates: verifies release at 0 stays at 0
- test_routing_table_updates_endpoint_state: verifies state actually changes
  after update and returns false for nonexistent endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
